generatedSentence: In a foreign, secluded outback, where archaic traditions held an undying grip, a uniquely voguish greyhound cult leader, unmatched in his godlike jiving, offered a biweekly backrub to the cowboys. His neoliberal policies were rubbish, but his soapy watermelon and crabmeat rathole stew kept the bacterial anchor from making problems reappear, while his eastward gaze scanned the armpit of the world. His idiosyncratic pharaoh-like walkups were famously farcical, a buffoon in a slicker, but an indicator of trouble as a tornado of imps, infected with nicotine haze, tangled the delivery routes. He dejectedly recalled how he’d enrolled in this limbo, carrying his backpacks, now feeling mawkish as a bollard of kachina spirits marched through the street, promising dubious goodies. Amidst the ruckus, a stinker of a kilometer-wide geographic jam, pawned off by rival brands, caused unequalled debauchery for gamers and farmers alike. The panda, a nifty laboratory accessory, suckled on a truffle, its viable commitment now tested as a mountain of jumbled junk, the development of a lifetime, became a mere vicissitude, lobster-like claws tearing at everything. Kings and kidnappers, in their multicolor jammies, heckled from the background, their muckraking point a puffy cloud over the www. The lover, offhand, saw how knifes and hammers in the kitchens facilitated a handful of core changes across the borders, building new wickets, a nephron of hope for the horses.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone figure wearing a patchwork slicker and a high-tech greyhound mask, accompanied by a small, mechanical panda. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The greyhound cult leader, mid-jive, offering a backrub to a cowboy in a dimly lit, secluded outback saloon, extreme wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The leader, now wearing a slicker and backpack, looks dejectedly at a swirling tornado of nicotine-hazed imps tangling a futuristic delivery drone, medium shot, high angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The mechanical panda, nibbling a truffle, sits amidst a jumbled mountain of junk and lobster-like debris, while gamers and farmers riot in the background, low angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Kings and kidnappers in colorful jammies heckling from a neon-lit border wall, while the protagonist watches horses being led through new wickets by figures with hammers and knives in their hands, wide shot.

definitions.definition: A foreign person, particularly:
A foreign ship.
Clipping of chamber foreign: an outhouse.
A foreign area, particularly:
Short for various phrases, including foreign language, foreign parts, and foreign service.
Located outside a country or place, especially one's own.
definitions.example: He liked visiting foreign cities.

definitions.definition: Originating from, characteristic of, belonging to, or being a citizen of a country or place other than the one under discussion.
definitions.example: There are many more foreign students in Europe since the Erasmus scheme started.

definitions.definition: Relating to a different nation.
definitions.example: foreign policy; foreign navies

definitions.definition: Not characteristic of or naturally taken in by an organism or system.
definitions.example: foreign body; foreign substance; foreign gene; foreign species

definitions.definition: (with to, formerly with from) Alien; strange.
definitions.example: It was completely foreign to their way of thinking.

definitions.definition: Held at a distance; excluded; exiled.
From a different one of the states of the United States, as of a state of residence or incorporation.
Belonging to a different organization, company etc.
definitions.example: My bank charges me $2.50 every time I use a foreign ATM.

definitions.definition: Outside, outdoors, outdoor.

(usually capitalized) A general term for the prehistoric period intermediate between the earliest period (‘Paleo-Indian’, ‘Paleo-American’, ‘American‐paleolithic’, &c.) of human presence in the Western Hemisphere, and the most recent prehistoric period (‘Woodland’, etc.).
(paleoanthropology) (A member of) an archaic variety of Homo sapiens.
Of or characterized by antiquity; old-fashioned, quaint, antiquated.
(of words) No longer in ordinary use, though still used occasionally to give a sense of antiquity.
Belonging to the archaic period
An agent that counteracts or neutralizes acidity, especially in the stomach.
Counteracting or neutralizing acidity, especially in the stomach.
To come back to life after having died.
To become undead.
Permanent; never-ending; infinite
definitions.example: Just doesn't understand

definitions.definition: In a unique manner.
definitions.example: a uniquely gifted musician

definitions.definition: To separate a matching pair.
(of a pair of things) not matched; odd
(of a single thing) not matched with anything else
Peerless; unrivalled; beyond comparison
A lean breed of dog used in hunting and racing.
A highball cocktail of vodka and grapefruit juice.
A swift steamer, especially an ocean steamer.
(of fish) To leap rapidly across the surface of the water.
Having the characteristics of a god.
Characteristic of a god.
A massage of the back.
To move or swing a weapon back and forth, particularly if demonstrating anger, threat or skill.
definitions.example: He brandished his sword at the pirates.

definitions.definition: To bear something with ostentatious show.
definitions.example: to brandish syllogisms

definitions.definition: Of, relating to, or caused by bacteria.
A tool used to moor a vessel to the bottom of a sea or river to resist movement.
An iron device so shaped as to grip the bottom and hold a vessel at her berth by the chain or rope attached. (FM 55-501).
The combined anchoring gear (anchor, rode, bill/peak and fittings such as bitts, cat, and windlass.)
Representation of the nautical tool, used as a heraldic charge.

Any instrument serving a purpose like that of a ship's anchor, such as an arrangement of timber to hold a dam fast; a device to hold the end of a bridge cable etc.; or a device used in metalworking to hold the core of a mould in place.
A marked point in a document that can be the target of a hyperlink.
An anchorman or anchorwoman.
The final runner in a relay race.
A point that is touched by the draw hand or string when the bow is fully drawn and ready to shoot.
A superstore or other facility that serves as a focus to bring customers into an area.
That which gives stability or security.
A metal tie holding adjoining parts of a building together.
Carved work, somewhat resembling an anchor or arrowhead; part of the ornaments of certain mouldings. It is seen in the echinus, or egg-and-anchor (called also egg-and-dart, egg-and-tongue) ornament.
One of the anchor-shaped spicules of certain sponges.
One of the calcareous spinules of certain holothurians, as in species of Synapta.
The thirty-fifth Lenormand card.
An anchorite or anchoress.
To reproach with contemptuous words; to deride, to mock, to taunt.
To say in a mocking or taunting manner.
To make a mocking remark or remarks; to jeer.
To accord or agree.
definitions.example: That explanation doesn’t jibe with the facts.

definitions.definition: Alternative spelling of gybe
To deceive; to be deceptive.
definitions.example: Don’t try to jive me! I know where you were last night!

definitions.definition: To dance.
To shut off or keep apart, as from company, society, etc.; withdraw (oneself) from society or into solitude.
To shut or keep out; exclude; preclude.
Hidden, isolated, remote.
A man who tends free-range cattle, especially in the American West.
A man who identifies with cowboy culture, including wearing a cowboy hat and being a fan of country and western music.
A person who engages in reckless behavior, especially for the purpose of showing off.
A dishonest and/or incompetent independent tradesman.
A playing card of king rank.
To work as a cowboy, herding cattle.
The meat of a crab.
A plant of the species Citrullus lanatus, bearing a melon-like fruit.
The fruit of the watermelon plant, having a green rind and watery flesh that is typically bright red when ripe and contains black pips.
An environmentalist with socialist leanings (from the similarity to the fruit, being green on the outside, and red (Communist) on the inside).
A pinkish-red colour, like that of watermelon flesh (also called watermelon pink).
An entrance to a living area or passageway used by mice or rats.
A living area used by mice or rats.
A particularly squalid human residence.
An area of a silo that has undergone ratholing, so that material moves mostly through the centre and accumulates around the edges.
A pigeonhole.
To hoard.
To take a conversation off topic, especially in technical meetings.
To surreptitiously or prematurely remove chips during a poker game.
(of material) to empty only in the center of a hopper or silo, persisting circumferentially.
Refuse, waste, garbage, junk, trash.
definitions.example: The rubbish is collected every Thursday in Gloucester, but on Wednesdays in Cheltenham.

definitions.definition: (by extension) An item, or items, of low quality.
definitions.example: Much of what they sell is rubbish.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Nonsense.
definitions.example: Everything the teacher said during that lesson was rubbish. How can she possibly think that a bass viol and a cello are the same thing?

definitions.definition: Debris or ruins of buildings.
To criticize, to denigrate, to denounce, to disparage.
Exceedingly bad; awful.
definitions.example: This has been a rubbish day, and it’s about to get worse: my mother-in-law is coming to stay.

definitions.definition: Used to express that something is exceedingly bad, awful, or terrible.
definitions.example:
The one day I actually practice my violin, the teacher cancels the lesson.
Aw, rubbish! Though at least this means you have time to play football.

definitions.definition: Used to express that what was recently said is nonsense or untrue; balderdash!, nonsense!
definitions.example: Rubbish! I did nothing of the sort!

definitions.definition: To appear again.
The most remote and desolate areas of Australia; the desert and areas too arid for growing crops.
Characteristic of the most remote and desolate areas of Australia; very remote from urban areas.
To or towards the most remote and desolate areas of Australia.
Something that is published or released once every two weeks.

definitions.definition: Occurring once every two weeks.
Occurring twice a week (but see the Usage notes).
Every two weeks.
Twice a week (but see the Usage notes).
The cavity beneath the junction of the arm and shoulder.
Somewhere or something considered unpleasant or undesirable.

definitions.definition: Elegant, fashionable or chic.
Trendy.
The direction or area lying to the east.
Situated or directed towards the east.
Towards the east.
Resembling soap.

Full of soap.

definitions.definition: Committing or involving flattery.

A customer who arrives without a reservation; a walk-in.

The card game faro.
A cradlesong, a soothing song to calm children or lull them to sleep.
To sing a lullaby to.
Peculiar to a specific individual; eccentric.
A violent windstorm characterized by a mobile, twisting, funnel-shaped cloud.

To cast down.
In a celebrated manner.

Really well, having great rapport

definitions.definition: Resembling a farce; ludicrous; absurd.
(Pueblo culture) A vaguely ancestral anthropomorphic spirit being, associated with clouds and rain or personifying the power in the sun, the earth or corn (among other things).
(Pueblo culture) A wooden doll, as might be given to a child, which represents such a being.
(Pueblo culture) A masked dancer who represents such a being in a ceremonial dance or masked ceremony.
Slippery or smooth due to a covering of liquid; often used to describe appearances.

definitions.definition: Appearing expensive or sophisticated.

definitions.definition: Superficially convincing but actually untrustworthy.

definitions.definition: (often used sarcastically) Clever, making an apparently hard task easy.

definitions.definition: Extraordinarily great or special.

definitions.definition: Sleek; smooth

Tobacco, cigarettes

(Roman Catholic theology, since circa 400 A.D.) The place where innocent souls exist temporarily until they can enter heaven, notably those of the saints who died before the advent of Christ (limbus patruum) and those of unbaptized but innocent children (limbus infantum).
(by extension, since the 16th century) Any in-between place, state or condition of neglect or oblivion which results in an unresolved status, delay or deadlock.
definitions.example: My application has been stuck in bureaucratic limbo for two weeks.

definitions.definition: To walk with long, regular strides, as a soldier does.

Said of people and things, and, when of people, usually in a bad sense

definitions.definition: Assisting a crime without actually participating in committing the crime itself.
Present in a minor amount, and not essential.

definitions.definition: Something difficult (e.g. a given puzzle) or unpleasant (e.g. negative review, nasty letter).

definitions.definition: Something of poor quality.

A hot day.

Pertaining to geography (or to geographics)
Determined by geography, as opposed to magnetic (i.e. North)
A conflagration; a flame.
A piece of burning wood or peat, or a glowing cinder.

A sword.
A mark or scar made by burning with a hot iron, especially to mark cattle or to classify the contents of a cask.
A branding iron.
The symbolic identity, represented by a name and/or a logo, which indicates a certain product or service to the public.
A specific product, service, or provider so distinguished.
definitions.example: Some brands of breakfast cereal contain a lot of sugar.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Any specific type or variety of something; a distinct style or manner.
definitions.example: I didn't appreciate his particular brand of flattery.

definitions.definition: The public image or reputation and recognized, typical style of an individual or group.
A mark of infamy; stigma.
Any minute fungus producing a burnt appearance in plants.

definitions.example: When they caught him, he was branded and then locked up.

definitions.definition: To mark (especially cattle) with a brand as proof of ownership.
definitions.example: The ranch hands had to brand every new calf by lunchtime.

definitions.definition: To make an indelible impression on the memory or senses.
definitions.example: Her face is branded upon my memory.

definitions.definition: To stigmatize, label (someone).
definitions.example: He was branded a fool by everyone that heard his story.

definitions.definition: To associate a product or service with a trademark or other name and related images.
definitions.example: They branded the new detergent "Suds-O", with a nature scene inside a green O on the muted-colored recycled-cardboard box.

definitions.definition: A person who works the land and/or who keeps livestock, especially on a farm.
Agent noun of farm; someone or something that farms.
One who takes taxes, customs, excise, or other duties, to collect for a certain rate per cent.

definitions.definition: The lord of the field, or one who farms the lot and cope of the crown.
To mix or confuse.
To meet or unite in a confused way.
definitions.example: I tried to study, but in my half-awake state, all of the concepts seemed to jumble together.

definitions.definition: In disarray, mixed up.
A sweet mixture of fruit boiled with sugar and allowed to congeal. Often spread on bread or toast or used in jam tarts.
A difficult situation.
Blockage, congestion.
definitions.example: A traffic jam caused us to miss the game's first period.

definitions.definition: An informal, impromptu performance or rehearsal.

(by extension) An informal event where people brainstorm and collaborate on projects.
To get something stuck in a confined space.
definitions.example: Her poor little baby toe got jammed in the door.

definitions.definition: To brusquely force something into a space; cram, squeeze.
definitions.example: The rush-hour train was jammed with commuters.

definitions.definition: To cause congestion or blockage. Often used with "up"
definitions.example: A single accident can jam the roads for hours.

definitions.definition: To block or confuse a broadcast signal.
To throw a pitch at or near the batter's hands.
definitions.example: Jones was jammed by the pitch.

definitions.definition: To play music (especially improvisation as a group, or an informal unrehearsed session).
To injure a finger or toe by sudden compression of the digit's tip.
definitions.example: When he tripped on the step he jammed his toe.

definitions.definition: To attempt to score points.
definitions.example: Toughie jammed four times in the second period.

definitions.definition: To bring (a vessel) so close to the wind that half her upper sails are laid aback.
To give up on a date or some joint endeavour; stand up, chicken out, jam out.
Without equal; unmatched.
Good, smart; useful or beneficial.
definitions.example: a nifty trick for reducing your household energy bill

definitions.definition: Stylish.
The red panda (Ailurus fulgens), a small raccoon-like animal of northeast Asia with reddish fur and a long, ringed tail.
Short for giant panda.
(law enforcement) Short for panda car.
A noisy disturbance and/or commotion.
A row, fight.
An individual act of debauchery.
An orgy.
To morally corrupt (someone); to seduce.
To debase (something); to lower the value of (something).
To indulge in revelry.
The process of developing; growth, directed change.
definitions.example: The development of this story has been slow.

definitions.definition: The process by which a mature multicellular organism or part of an organism is produced by the addition of new cells.
definitions.example: The organism has reached a crucial stage in its development.

definitions.definition: Something which has developed.
definitions.example: Our news team brings you the latest developments.

definitions.definition: A project consisting of one or more commercial or residential buildings.
The building of such a project.
The application of new ideas to practical problems (cf. research).
definitions.example: Our development department has produced three new adhesives this year.

definitions.definition: The active placement of the pieces, or the process of achieving it.
definitions.example: White's development is good, but black's has been hampered by the pawn on e5.

definitions.definition: The process by in which previous material is transformed and restated.
The second section of a piece of music in sonata form, in which the original theme is revisited in altered and varying form.
The expression of a function in the form of a series.
Discarded or waste material; rubbish, trash.
A collection of miscellaneous items of little value.
Any narcotic drug, especially heroin.
The genitalia.
Salt beef.
Pieces of old cable or cordage, used for making gaskets, mats, swabs, etc., and when picked to pieces, forming oakum for filling the seams of ships.
A fragment of any solid substance; a thick piece; a chunk.
Material or resources of a kind lacking commercial value.

definitions.definition: To throw away.
To find something for very little money (meaning derived from the term junk shop)
definitions.example: (On Facebook, a record collector wrote:) "The newest addition to my Annette Hanshaw collection, I junked this beautiful flawless E-copy within walking distance from my house."

definitions.definition: Regular change or succession from one thing to another, or one part of a cycle to the next; alternation; mutual succession; interchange.
(often in the plural) A change, especially in one's life or fortunes.
An organism that is able to live and develop.
Able to live on its own (as for a newborn).
Able to be done, possible.
Able to live and develop.
To give suck to; to nurse at the breast, udder, or dugs.
To nurse; to suck milk from a nursing mother.
To nurse from (a breast, nursing mother, etc.).
The act or an instance of committing, putting in charge, keeping, or trust, especially:
Promise or agreement to do something in the future, especially:
Being bound emotionally or intellectually to a course of action or to another person or persons.
The trait of sincerity and focused purpose.
Perpetration as in a crime or mistake.
State of being pledged or engaged.
The act of being locked away, such as in an institution for the mentally ill or in jail.
A room, building or institution equipped for scientific research, experimentation or analysis.
A place where chemicals, drugs or microbes are prepared or manufactured.
A confection having a center of ganache and an outer coating of powdered cocoa or chocolate.
Any of various edible fungi, of the genus Tuber, that grow in the soil in southern Europe; the earthnut.
An elevation of land of considerable dimensions rising more or less abruptly, forming a conspicuous figure in the landscape, usually having a small extent of surface at its summit.
definitions.example: We spent the weekend hiking in the mountains.

definitions.definition: Something very large in size or quantity; a huge amount; a great heap.
definitions.example: He was a real mountain of a man, standing seven feet tall.

definitions.definition: Of, belonging to, relating to, or found on a mountain; like a mountain in size; (of living things) growing or living on a mountain.
definitions.example: some mountain ranges; the high mountain top; a scenic mountain view; the mountain lodge; the leaves of mountain cranberries; a mountain goat

definitions.definition: A difficult task or challenge.
A woman's large breast.
The twenty-first Lenormand card.
Wine made from grapes that grow on a mountain.
To search for and expose corruption or scandal, especially as a form of investigative journalism.
A display of many colors.
definitions.example: a stamp printed in multicolor

definitions.definition: Having, resembling, or pertaining to many colors.
One's social heritage, or previous life; what one did in the past.
definitions.example: The lawyer had a background in computer science.

definitions.definition: A part of the picture that depicts scenery to the rear or behind the main subject; context.
Information relevant to the current situation about past events; history.
A less important feature of scenery (as opposed to foreground).
definitions.example: The photographer let us pick a background for the portrait.

definitions.definition: The image or color over which a computer's desktop items are shown (e.g. icons or application windows).
A type of activity on a computer that is not normally visible to the user.
definitions.example: The antivirus program is running in the background.

definitions.definition: To put in a position that is not prominent.
To gather and provide background information (on).
Less important or less noticeable in a scene or system.
definitions.example: The antivirus program runs on a background thread.

definitions.definition: Pajamas/pyjamas
A gun.
A tool with a heavy head and a handle used for pounding.
definitions.example: Bobby used a hammer and nails to fix the two planks together

definitions.definition: The act of using a hammer to hit something}}
definitions.example: The nail is too loose - give it a hammer.

definitions.definition: A moving part of a firearm that strikes the firing pin to discharge a gun.
The malleus, a small bone of the middle ear.
In a piano or dulcimer, a piece of wood covered in felt that strikes the string.
definitions.example: The sound the piano makes comes from the hammers striking the strings

definitions.definition: A device made of a heavy steel ball attached to a length of wire, and used for throwing.
The last stone in an end.
(frisbee) A frisbee throwing style in which the disc is held upside-down with a forehand grip and thrown above the head.
Part of a clock that strikes upon a bell to indicate the hour.
One who, or that which, smites or shatters.
definitions.example: St. Augustine was the hammer of heresies.

definitions.definition: To strike repeatedly with a hammer, some other implement, the fist, etc.
definitions.example: Tony hammered on the door to try to get him to open.

definitions.definition: To form or forge with a hammer; to shape by beating.
To emphasize a point repeatedly.
To hit particularly hard.
To ride very fast.
To strike internally, as if hit by a hammer.
definitions.example: I could hear the engine’s valves hammering once the timing rod was thrown.

definitions.definition: To defeat (a person, a team) resoundingly
definitions.example: We hammered them 5-0!

definitions.definition: To make high demands on (a system or service).
To declare (a person) a defaulter on the stock exchange.
To beat down the price of (a stock), or depress (a market).
To have hard sex with
definitions.example: Daniel hammered Mary til he came.

definitions.definition: To question harshly in an attempt to find or reveal weaknesses.
To insult, tease, make fun of or badger.
definitions.example: Promise that you won't heckle me after my performance.

definitions.definition: To prepare flax for spinning using special combs called hackles
Any of several animals related to Equus ferus caballus.
Equipment with legs.
Type of equipment.

A mass of earthy matter, or rock of the same character as the wall rock, occurring in the course of a vein, as of coal or ore; hence, to take horse (said of a vein) is to divide into branches for a distance.
The sedative, antidepressant, and anxiolytic drug morphine, chiefly when used illicitly.

An informal variant of basketball in which players match shots made by their opponent(s), each miss adding a letter to the word "horse", with 5 misses spelling the whole word and eliminating a player, until only the winner is left. Also HORSE, H-O-R-S-E or H.O.R.S.E. (see Variations of basketball#H-O-R-S-E).
(among students) A translation or other illegitimate aid in study or examination.
(among students) horseplay; tomfoolery
To frolic, to act mischievously. (Usually followed by "around".)
To provide with a horse; supply horses for.
To get on horseback.
To sit astride of; to bestride.
(of a male horse) To copulate with (a mare).
To take or carry on the back.
To place (someone) on the back of another person, or on a wooden horse, etc., to be flogged; (hence) to flog.
To urge at work tyrannically.
To charge for work before it is finished.
A vessel for boiling a liquid or cooking food, usually metal and equipped with a lid.
definitions.example: There's a hot kettle of soup on the stove.

definitions.definition: The quantity held by a kettle.
A vessel for boiling water for tea.
definitions.example: Stick the kettle on and we'll have a nice cup of tea.

definitions.definition: A kettle hole, sometimes any pothole.
(collective) A group of raptors riding a thermal, especially when migrating.
definitions.example: a kettle of hawks

definitions.definition: A steam locomotive
A kettledrum.
An instance of kettling; a group of protesters or rioters confined in a limited area.
A kind of fishweir resembling a wattle or fence.
A male monarch; a man who heads a monarchy. If it's an absolute monarchy, then he is the supreme ruler of his nation.
definitions.example: Henry VIII was the king of England from 1509 to 1547.

definitions.definition: A powerful or majorly influential person.
definitions.example: Howard Stern styled himself as the "king of all media".

definitions.definition: Something that has a preeminent position.
definitions.example: In times of financial panic, cash is king.

definitions.definition: A component of certain games.
A king skin.
definitions.example: Oi mate, have you got kings?

definitions.definition: A male dragonfly; a drake.
A king-sized bed.
A male person.
To crown king, to make (a person) king.
To rule over as king.
To perform the duties of a king.
To assume or pretend preeminence (over); to lord it over.
To promote a piece of draughts/checkers that has traversed the board to the opposite side, that piece subsequently being permitted to move backwards as well as forwards.
To dress and perform as a drag king.
A drinking game that uses playing cards.
A sounding stone, a Chinese musical instrument.
A person who kidnaps someone.
The basic structural and functional unit of the kidney, which filters the blood in order to regulate chemical concentrations, and thereby produces urine.
A small door or gate, especially one beside a larger one.
A small window or other opening, sometimes fitted with a grating.
A service window, as in a bank or train station, where a customer conducts transactions with a teller; a ticket barrier at a rail station, box office at a cinema, etc.
One of the two wooden structures at each end of the pitch, consisting of three vertical stumps and two bails; the target for the bowler, defended by the batsman.
A dismissal; the act of a batsman getting out.
The period during which two batsmen bat together.
The pitch.
The area around the stumps where the batsmen stand.
Any of the small arches through which the balls are driven.
A temporary metal attachment that one attaches one's lift-ticket to.
A shelter made from tree boughs, used by lumbermen.
The space between the pillars, in post-and-stall working.
An angle bracket when used in HTML.
Usually preceded by the: collectively, all of the web pages on the Internet stored in different computers around the world that hyperlink to each other and to other kinds of documents and media.
Internet resources that are retrieved by Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP).
One who loves and cares for another person in a romantic way; a sweetheart, love, soulmate, boyfriend, girlfriend or spouse.
A sexual partner, especially one with whom someone is having an affair.
A person who loves something.
definitions.example: a lover of fine wines

definitions.definition: (with "my") An informal term of address for any friend.
definitions.example: All right, me lover?

definitions.definition: Without planning or thinking ahead.
definitions.example: She gave an offhand speech.

definitions.definition: Careless; without sufficient thought or consideration.
definitions.example: He doesn't realise how hurtful his offhand remarks can be.

definitions.definition: Curt, abrupt, unfriendly.
definitions.example: She was quite offhand with me yesterday.

definitions.definition: Right away, immediately, without thinking about it.
definitions.example: Offhand, I'd guess that that's a yellow-bellied sapsucker.

definitions.definition: In an offhand manner.
The outer edge of something.
definitions.example: the borders of the garden

definitions.definition: A decorative strip around the edge of something.
definitions.example: There's a nice frilly border around the picture frame.

definitions.definition: A strip of ground in which ornamental plants are grown.
The line or frontier area separating political or geographical regions.
definitions.example: The border between Canada and USA is the longest in the world.

definitions.definition: Border morris or border dancing; a vigorous style of traditional English dance originating from villages along the border between England and Wales, performed by a team of dancers usually with their faces disguised with black makeup.
A string that is both a prefix and a suffix of another particular string.
To put a border on something.
To form a border around; to bound.
To lie on, or adjacent to, a border of.
definitions.example: Denmark borders Germany to the south.

definitions.definition: To touch at a border (with on, upon, or with).
definitions.example: Connecticut borders on Massachusetts.

definitions.definition: To approach; to come near to; to verge (with on or upon).
To make easy or easier.
To help bring about.
To preside over (a meeting, a seminar).
The amount that a hand will grasp or contain.
A hand's breadth; four inches.
A small number, usually approximately five.
A group or number of things; a bunch.
Something which can only be managed with difficulty.
definitions.example: Those twins are a real handful to look after.

definitions.definition: A five-year prison sentence.
A room or area for preparing food.
Cuisine.
The nape of a person's hairline, often referring to its uncombed or "nappy" look.
The percussion section of an orchestra.
A utensil for roasting meat.
A domesticated or uneducated form of a language.
A public gaming room in a casino.
Anything eaten as a relish with bread, potatoes, etc.
To do kitchen work; to prepare food.
To embellish a basic food; to season, add condiments, etc.
(by extension) To embellish; to dress up.
To cut with a knife.
To use a knife to injure or kill by stabbing, slashing, or otherwise using the sharp edge of the knife as a weapon.
To cut through as if with a knife.
To betray, especially in the context of a political slate.
To positively ignore, especially in order to denigrate. compare cut
The central part of fruit, containing the kernels or seeds.
definitions.example: the core of an apple or quince

definitions.definition: The heart or inner part of a physical thing
The center or inner part of a space or area
definitions.example: the core of a square

definitions.definition: The most important part of a thing; the essence.
definitions.example: the core of a subject

definitions.definition: The portion of a mold that creates an internal cavity within a casting or that makes a hole in or through a casting.
The bony process which forms the central axis of the horns in many animals.
Magnetic data storage.
An individual computer processor, in the sense when several processors (called cores or CPU cores) are plugged together in one single integrated circuit to work as one (called multi-core processor).
definitions.example: I wanted to play a particular computer game, which required I buy a new computer, so while the game said it needed at least a dual-core processor, I wanted my computer to be a bit ahead of the curve, so I bought a quad-core.

definitions.definition: The material between surface materials in a structured composite sandwich material.
definitions.example: a floor panel with a Nomex honeycomb core

definitions.definition: The inner part of a nuclear reactor in which the nuclear reaction takes place.
A piece of ferromagnetic material (i.e. soft iron), inside the windings of an electromagnet, that channels the magnetic field.
A disorder of sheep caused by worms in the liver.
A cylindrical sample of rock or other materials obtained by core drilling.
A tiny sample of organic material obtained by means of a fine-needle biopsy.
The central part of a protein structure consisting in mostly hydrophobic aminoacids.
The set of feasible allocations that cannot be improved upon by a subset (a coalition) of the economy's agents.
A hollow cylindrical piece of cardboard around which a web of paper or plastic is winded.
An atomic nucleus plus inner electrons (i.e. except valence electrons).
To remove the core of an apple or other fruit.
To extract a sample with a drill.
A discrete division of something.
A sharp extremity.
One of the several different parts of the escutcheon.
A short piece of cordage used in reefing sails.
A string or lace used to tie together certain garments.
Lace worked by the needle.
definitions.example: point de Venise; Brussels point

definitions.definition: An item of private information; a hint; a tip; a pointer.
The attitude assumed by a pointer dog when he finds game.
definitions.example: The dog came to a point.

definitions.definition: The perpendicular rising of a hawk over the place where its prey has gone into cover.
The act of pointing, as of the foot downward in certain dance positions.
The gesture of extending the index finger in a direction in order to indicate something.
A vaccine point.
In various sports, a position of a certain player, or, by extension, the player occupying that position.
Swollen or inflated in shape, as if filled with air; pillow-like.
Coming or exhaling in puffs.
definitions.example: A puffy fat man in a tracksuit came jogging around the corner.

definitions.definition: Speaking or writing in an exaggeratedly eloquent and self-important manner.
